$comment: PL-3 diegetic seed. The run seed is COMPOSED from three choices the player makes during the pre-place 'consciousness' beats — one word from each lane (adjective, adjective, noun). The composed phrase (e.g. 'gilded-restless-tide') IS the seed; it is never shown. Each word is paired with a fragment of evocative, place-agnostic prose — the emerging threads of a not-yet-born consciousness — so the player authors the seed by feeling, not by picking a word from a list. 9 x 9 x 9 = 729 distinct authored seeds.

first.prompt: Before there is a name, before there is a place, there is only a quality to the dark — a first temperature of being. It is…

words.fragment: warm, and faintly gold, the way light feels through closed eyelids.
never still, a tide already pulling toward some far shore.
hard-edged and cold, a thing that will not bend easily.
an ache that wants, before it even knows what wanting is.
bright beyond reason, certain it was made to be seen.
slow and deep, a quiet that can outwait the world.
untamed, all teeth and instinct, answering to no one yet.
soft at the center, easily wounded, easily moved.
already reaching upward, as if the dark itself were a ceiling to break.

second.prompt: And beneath that first quality, a second nature stirs — the grain that runs through everything that will follow. It is…

words.fragment: a sideways mind, finding the seam in every wall.
turned toward something larger, hungry for a thing to believe.
willing, even now, to let what must fall, fall.
made to draw the eye and hold the room.
set like stone, slow to forgive and slower to forget.
open-handed, giving warmth away before it is asked.
a depth that turns inward, weighing every shadow.
loud and unashamed, certain that boldness is its own reward.
counting, always counting, the true cost of every thing.

third.prompt: And then — the first image. Not a memory; a premonition. The shape your life will circle back to, again and again. It is…

words.fragment: water without end, rising and falling, carrying everything away and back.
something built impossibly tall, your name cut into its highest stone.
a field heavy with what was sown, and the long work of bringing it in.
a circle of weight and light, and the heads it will pass between.
fire and hammer, the making of hard things from raw ones.
a promise older than you, that you are somehow bound to keep.
everything on the table, and the held breath before the turn.
a leaving, a long road away from one world toward another.
a line of faces stretching forward past the edge of sight — yours among them.
